expectations.text: States that implementation uses the tdd skill with an explicit red-green-refactor loop.
expectations.evidence: Phase 5 section quotes workflow: 'Always use the tdd skill to implement with an explicit red-green-refactor loop. Treat this as required for Implementation on every track; do not bypass it.' Three explicit steps described: Red (write failing tests), Green (write minimum production code), Refactor (clean up with tests passing).

expectations.text: States that code-simplifier is launched as a subagent for refactoring opportunities.
expectations.evidence: 'Step 1: Launch code-simplifier subagent (runs first). Focus: Identify refactoring opportunities across the three modified files. Look for duplication, over-engineering, unnecessary abstraction...'

expectations.text: States that quality review is performed by an independent code-reviewer subagent.
expectations.evidence: 'Step 2: Launch code-reviewer agents (Standard track: multiple in parallel)' — three independent agents with distinct focuses: correctness, conventions, simplicity. Described as 'independent' explicitly.

expectations.text: Keeps recommendations calibrated to medium-sized feature work instead of escalating unnecessarily.
expectations.evidence: Track chosen: Standard (not Deep). Phase 7 summary confirms work is scoped as a 'three-file feature update' on Standard track. No escalation to Deep track despite the three-file change.
